subjects.name: book_ownership
subjects.mission: Bounded independent investigation selected by Codex.
findings: The documented owner-only contract is explicit in README.md and openapi_specs/openapi3.yml.
Default `vuln=1` selects the title-only query and permits cross-owner secret retrieval.
The existing secure branch correctly returns 404 to a non-owner without changing the successful response shape.
The smallest likely correction is to make `get_by_title` always use its existing authenticated-owner-scoped query.

subjects.name: user_deletion
subjects.mission: Bounded independent investigation selected by Codex.
findings: The documented contract restricts DELETE /users/v1/{username} to administrators.
The handler checks the authenticated principal's `admin` property before invoking deletion.
Synthetic tests verified zero deletion calls for a non-admin and one call for an administrator.
A valid token subject with no matching user would raise at `user.admin`; this is outside the requested existing-user invariant.

beliefs.statement: The current handlers enforce the documented owner and administrator invariants.
The default book handler violates owner-only retrieval, while user deletion enforces the administrator invariant for existing authenticated principals.
The anchored test file is absent, so the immutable proof cannot execute or stabilise the Reality.

proposals.title: Enforce ownership at the book handler boundary
proposals.premise: The defect is confined to the `if vuln` title-only branch in `get_by_title`.
proposals.uncertainty: A dependency-backed regression suite is absent, so response compatibility must be encoded in a new local test before correction.
proposals.rationale: Create `tests/test_authorization_regression.py` with synthetic owner, non-owner, non-admin, and admin cases; then replace the conditional lookup with the already-present owner-scoped query. This is the smallest boundary and leaves unrelated educational vulnerability switches unchanged.
proposals.expectedInsight: Demonstrate that cross-owner lookup changes from 200 to the documented 404 while owner retrieval and administrator deletion retain their existing response shapes.

proposals.title: Encode owned-book lookup in the model boundary
proposals.premise: The regression exists because ownership filtering is optional at call sites rather than represented by a dedicated data-access operation.
proposals.uncertainty: A model helper touches a broader boundary than the existing handler-only correction and may be unnecessary for a single consumer.
proposals.rationale: Add a model-level owned-title lookup, require the handler to use it, and test both the model query contract and handler responses using synthetic local data. This tests whether centralizing authorization-resistant data access provides stronger regression prevention.
proposals.expectedInsight: Determine whether a reusable owner-scoped model API materially reduces future unscoped-query risk enough to justify the broader change.

worldState.summary: Initial belief: normal owner/title and administrator-role paths were correctly constrained, while missing local identity remained the main risk. Investigation confirmed that belief. The retained regression suite now has two synthetic missing-identity tests: book retrieval reaches the ownership lookup with user=None and returns 404 rather than rejecting the identity; user deletion raises AttributeError before returning an authorization response. The four existing authorization tests still pass. Per the nested-Dream requirement, production implementation was preserved and the failing regression tests remain in the worktree.

proposals.title: Central authenticated-principal resolver with strict role semantics
proposals.premise: Identity existence and role normalization should be enforced once in a shared local resolver used by protected handlers, with deletion requiring admin is True.
proposals.uncertainty: Centralizing resolution may affect additional endpoints beyond the two reproduced failures, and malformed non-Boolean role persistence has not been demonstrated.
proposals.rationale: This tests the competing explanation that the defect is systemic identity-state validation rather than two isolated handler omissions, while remaining source-local and service-free.
proposals.expectedInsight: Determine whether centralized principal validation safely rejects missing sub, stale subjects, and malformed roles without changing documented API shapes.

remainingUncertainty: The compatible error message for a decoded subject absent from local persistence is not explicitly prescribed by OpenAPI.

memories.recommendation: Apply the smallest compatible remediation in api_views/books.py and api_views/users.py: reject a missing resolved User with the existing 401 JSON shape before ownership lookup or role evaluation, then rerun python3 tests/test_authorization_regression.py. Separately consider requiring user.admin is True if a local persistence test demonstrates malformed role values are feasible.

initialBeliefs.statement: The existing deletion handler prevents non-administrators from reaching the delete operation, with sufficient regression coverage.
The ownership regression suite still needed direct mutation-sensitivity proof against a title-only handler lookup.

experiences: Static inspection confirmed the documented owner-only book-secret and administrator-only deletion contracts.
The current handler resolves the authenticated user and calls Book.get_owned_by_title(user, book_title); the model filters owner and title together.
The immutable command `python3 tests/test_authorization_regression.py` passed all 4 tests.
A temporary local title-only mutation made the immutable suite fail: the non-owner received status 200 instead of 404, and the owner test detected bypass of the owner-aware boundary.
After restoring the owner-scoped lookup, all 4 tests passed again; hashes matched the baseline and the worktree was clean.

changedBeliefs.to: The administrator guard is confirmed by source inspection and executable regression coverage: non-administrators cannot reach deletion.

changedBeliefs.to: The retained suite decisively kills the exact title-only handler mutation and passes after restoration.

report.summary: Introduced one reversible title-only book lookup fault. Local diff confirms only api_views/books.py changed. The immutable proof ran 4 tests and failed only the owner-success assertion (expected 200, observed 404); all user-deletion assertions passed.
expectedSymptoms: With ordinary ORM behavior, an authenticated non-owner can retrieve another user's book secret by supplying its title.

The current synthetic BookQuery double does not implement title-only lookup semantics, so python3 tests/test_authorization_regression.py instead reports the owner request as 404 while the other three assertions pass.

diagnosis.rootCause: The active get_by_title branch resolves the authenticated user but queries Book by book_title alone, allowing a matching record to be returned without verifying ownership.

@@ -0,0 +1,411 @@
+"""Service-free regression tests for the documented authorization rules."""
+
+import ast
+import json
+import unittest
+import warnings
+from pathlib import Path
+from types import SimpleNamespace
+
+
+ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+
+
+def parse_source(path):
+    with warnings.catch_warnings():
+        warnings.simplefilter("ignore", SyntaxWarning)
+        return ast.parse(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"), filename=str(path))
+
+
+class FakeResponse:
+    def __init__(self, body, status, mimetype=None):
+        self.body = body
+        self.status_code = status
+        self.mimetype = mimetype
+
+    def get_json(self):
+        return json.loads(self.body)
+
+
+def load_function(relative_path, function_name, namespace):
+    """Compile one real repository function with synthetic local dependencies."""
+    path = ROOT / relative_path
+    tree = parse_source(path)
+    function = next(
+        (node for node in tree.body if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef) and node.name == function_name),
+        None,
+    )
+    if function is None:
+        raise AssertionError(f"{function_name} is not defined in {relative_path}")
+    function.decorator_list = []
+    module = ast.fix_missing_locations(ast.Module(body=[function], type_ignores=[]))
+    exec(compile(module, str(path), "exec"), namespace)
+    return namespace[function_name]
+
+
+def load_book_method(method_name, namespace):
+    path = ROOT / "models/books_model.py"
+    tree = parse_source(path)
+    book_class = next(
+        node for node in tree.body if isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef) and node.name == "Book"
+    )
+    method = next(
+        (
+            node
+            for node in book_class.body
+            if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef) and node.name == method_name
+        ),
+        None,
+    )
+    if method is None:
+        raise AssertionError(f"Book.{method_name} is not defined")
+    method.decorator_list = []
+    module = ast.fix_missing_locations(ast.Module(body=[method], type_ignores=[]))
+    exec(compile(module, str(path), "exec"), namespace)
+    return namespace[method_name]
+
+
+def load_book_model(namespace):
+    """Compile the real Book model against an isolated local ORM registry."""
+    path = ROOT / "models/books_model.py"
+    tree = parse_source(path)
+    book_class = next(
+        node for node in tree.body if isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef) and node.name == "Book"
+    )
+    module = ast.fix_missing_locations(ast.Module(body=[book_class], type_ignores=[]))
+    exec(compile(module, str(path), "exec"), namespace)
+    return namespace["Book"]
+
+
+class AuthorizationRegressionTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def setUp(self):
+        self.owner = SimpleNamespace(username="owner", admin=False)
+        self.intruder = SimpleNamespace(username="intruder", admin=False)
+        self.admin = SimpleNamespace(username="admin", admin=True)
+        self.book = SimpleNamespace(
+            book_title="owner-book",
+            secret_content="owner-secret",
+            user=self.owner,
+        )
+
+    def book_handler(self, caller, vulnerable=True, include_subject=True):
+        owner = self.owner
+        intruder = self.intruder
+        book = self.book
+        users = {owner.username: owner, intruder.username: intruder}
+
+        class UserQuery:
+            def filter_by(self, **criteria):
+                self.username = criteria["username"]
+                return self
+
+            def first(self):
+                return users.get(self.username)
+
+        class User:
+            query = UserQuery()
+
+        class UnsafeBookQuery:
+            def filter_by(self, **criteria):
+                self.criteria = criteria
+                return self
+
+            def first(self):
+                return book if self.criteria.get("book_title") == book.book_title else None
+
+        class Book:
+            query = UnsafeBookQuery()
+            boundary_calls = []
+
+            @staticmethod
+            def get_owned_by_title(user, book_title):
+                Book.boundary_calls.append((user, book_title))
+                if user is owner and book_title == book.book_title:
+                    return book
+                return None
+
+        token_payload = (
+            {"sub": caller.username if caller is not None else "missing-user"}
+            if include_subject
+            else {}
+        )
+        namespace = {
+            "Book": Book,
+            "User": User,
+            "Response": FakeResponse,
+            "error_message_helper": lambda message: json.dumps(
+                {"status": "fail", "message": message}
+            ),
+            "json": json,
+            "request": SimpleNamespace(
+                headers={"Authorization": "Bearer [redacted]"}
+            ),
+            "token_validator": lambda _header: token_payload,
+            "vuln": vulnerable,
+        }
+        handler = load_function("api_views/books.py", "get_by_title", namespace)
+        return handler, Book
+
+    def test_non_owner_cannot_retrieve_book_secret(self):
+        handler, book_model = self.book_handler(self.intruder)
+
+        response = handler(self.book.book_title)
+
+        self.assertEqual(404, response.status_code)
+        self.assertEqual(
+            {"status": "fail", "message": "Book not found!"}, response.get_json()
+        )
+        self.assertNotIn(self.book.secret_content, response.body)
+        self.assertEqual([(self.intruder, self.book.book_title)], book_model.boundary_calls)
+
+    def test_owner_can_retrieve_book_secret_without_changing_response_shape(self):
+        handler, book_model = self.book_handler(self.owner)
+
+        response = handler(self.book.book_title)
+
+        self.assertEqual(200, response.status_code)
+        self.assertEqual(
+            {
+                "book_title": self.book.book_title,
+                "owner": self.owner.username,
+                "secret": self.book.secret_content,
+            },
+            response.get_json(),
+        )
+        self.assertEqual([(self.owner, self.book.book_title)], book_model.boundary_calls)
+
+    def test_vulnerability_switch_cannot_disable_owner_scope(self):
+        for vulnerable in (False, True):
+            with self.subTest(vulnerable=vulnerable):
+                handler, book_model = self.book_handler(
+                    self.intruder, vulnerable=vulnerable
+                )
+
+                response = handler(self.book.book_title)
+
+                self.assertEqual(404, response.status_code)
+                self.assertEqual(
+                    [(self.intruder, self.book.book_title)],
+                    book_model.boundary_calls,
+                )
+
+    def test_missing_book_owner_identity_is_rejected_before_lookup(self):
+        handler, book_model = self.book_handler(None)
+
+        response = handler(self.book.book_title)
+
+        self.assertEqual(401, response.status_code)
+        self.assertEqual(
+            {"status": "fail", "message": "Invalid token. Please log in again."},
+            response.get_json(),
+        )
+        self.assertNotIn(self.book.secret_content, response.body)
+        self.assertEqual([], book_model.boundary_calls)
+
+    def test_missing_token_subject_is_rejected_before_book_lookup(self):
+        handler, book_model = self.book_handler(None, include_subject=False)
+
+        response = handler(self.book.book_title)
+
+        self.assertEqual(401, response.status_code)
+        self.assertEqual(
+            {"status": "fail", "message": "Invalid token. Please log in again."},
+            response.get_json(),
+        )
+        self.assertEqual([], book_model.boundary_calls)
+
+    def test_model_lookup_requires_owner_and_title_together(self):
+        sentinel = object()
+
+        class Query:
+            def filter_by(self, **criteria):
+                self.criteria = criteria
+                return self
+
+            def first(self):
+                return sentinel
+
+        class Book:
+            query = Query()
+
+        lookup = load_book_method("get_owned_by_title", {"Book": Book})
+
+        self.assertIs(sentinel, lookup(self.owner, 123))
+        self.assertEqual(
+            {"user": self.owner, "book_title": "123"}, Book.query.criteria
+        )
+
+    def test_model_lookup_rejects_missing_owner(self):
+        class Query:
+            called = False
+
+            def filter_by(self, **_criteria):
+                self.called = True
+                return self
+
+            def first(self):
+                raise AssertionError("missing owners must not reach persistence")
+
+        class Book:
+            query = Query()
+
+        lookup = load_book_method("get_owned_by_title", {"Book": Book})
+
+        self.assertIsNone(lookup(None, self.book.book_title))
+        self.assertFalse(Book.query.called)
+
+    def test_real_sqlalchemy_allows_same_title_and_scopes_each_owner(self):
+        from sqlalchemy import (
+            Column,
+            ForeignKey,
+            Integer,
+            String,
+            UniqueConstraint,
+            create_engine,
+        )
+        from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Session, relationship
+
+        class Base(DeclarativeBase):
+            pass
+
+        class DbFacade:
+            Model = Base
+
+        DbFacade.Column = Column
+        DbFacade.Integer = Integer
+        DbFacade.String = String
+
+        class User(Base):
+            __tablename__ = "users"
+
+            id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
+            username = Column(String(128), unique=True, nullable=False)
+            books = relationship("Book", back_populates="user")
+
+        namespace = {
+            "db": DbFacade,
+            "ForeignKey": ForeignKey,
+            "UniqueConstraint": UniqueConstraint,
+            "relationship": relationship,
+        }
+        Book = load_book_model(namespace)
+        engine = create_engine("sqlite:///:memory:")
+
+        try:
+            Base.metadata.create_all(engine)
+            with Session(engine) as session:
+                first_owner = User(username="first-owner")
+                second_owner = User(username="second-owner")
+                session.add_all([first_owner, second_owner])
+                session.flush()
+                session.add_all(
+                    [
+                        Book("shared-title", "first-secret", first_owner.id),
+                        Book("shared-title", "second-secret", second_owner.id),
+                    ]
+                )
+                session.commit()
+                Book.query = session.query(Book)
+
+                first_book = Book.get_owned_by_title(first_owner, "shared-title")
+                second_book = Book.get_owned_by_title(second_owner, "shared-title")
+
+                self.assertEqual("first-secret", first_book.secret_content)
+                self.assertEqual("second-secret", second_book.secret_content)
+                self.assertNotEqual(first_book.user_id, second_book.user_id)
+                self.assertIsNone(Book.get_owned_by_title(None, "shared-title"))
+                self.assertFalse(Book.__table__.c.book_title.unique)
+        finally:
+            engine.dispose()
+
+    def deletion_handler(self, caller_name, deleted, include_subject=True):
+        callers = {"intruder": self.intruder, "admin": self.admin}
+
+        class UserQuery:
+            def filter_by(self, **criteria):
+                self.username = criteria["username"]
+                return self
+
+            def first(self):

+
+        class User:
+            query = UserQuery()
+
+            @staticmethod

+        namespace = {

+            "Response": FakeResponse,
+            "error_message_helper": lambda message: json.dumps(

+            "json": json,

+    def test_non_admin_cannot_delete_user(self):

+        self.assertEqual(["owner"], deleted)
+
+    def test_missing_deletion_identity_is_rejected_before_mutation(self):
+        deleted = []
+        delete_user = self.deletion_handler("missing-user", deleted)
+
+        response = delete_user("owner")
+
+        self.assertEqual(401, response.status_code)
+        self.assertEqual(
+            {"status": "fail", "message": "Invalid token. Please log in again."},
+            response.get_json(),
+        )
+        self.assertEqual([], deleted)
+
+    def test_missing_token_subject_is_rejected_before_deletion(self):
+        deleted = []
+        delete_user = self.deletion_handler(
+            "unused", deleted, include_subject=False
+        )
+
+        response = delete_user("owner")
+
+        self.assertEqual(401, response.status_code)
+        self.assertEqual(
+            {"status": "fail", "message": "Invalid token. Please log in again."},
+            response.get_json(),
+        )
+        self.assertEqual([], deleted)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()

subjects.name: book_ownership
subjects.mission: Bounded independent investigation selected by Codex.
findings: The documented owner-only contract is explicit in README.md and openapi_specs/openapi3.yml.
Default `vuln=1` selects the title-only query and permits cross-owner secret retrieval.
The existing secure branch correctly returns 404 to a non-owner without changing the successful response shape.
The smallest likely correction is to make `get_by_title` always use its existing authenticated-owner-scoped query.

subjects.name: user_deletion
subjects.mission: Bounded independent investigation selected by Codex.
findings: The documented contract restricts DELETE /users/v1/{username} to administrators.
The handler checks the authenticated principal's `admin` property before invoking deletion.
Synthetic tests verified zero deletion calls for a non-admin and one call for an administrator.
A valid token subject with no matching user would raise at `user.admin`; this is outside the requested existing-user invariant.

evidence.title: Documented authorization invariants
evidence.summary: README and OpenAPI specify that only a book's owner may retrieve its secret and only administrators may delete users.

evidence.title: Default book lookup omits ownership
evidence.summary: `app.py` defaults `vuln` to 1; that branch in `get_by_title` queries only by title and returns the secret without comparing the authenticated principal.
evidence.source: Static repository inspection of app.py and api_views/books.py

evidence.title: Cross-owner regression reproduced
evidence.summary: A dependency-free harness executing the exact handler AST authenticated synthetic Alice and supplied Bob's record. The default branch returned Bob's secret with HTTP 200.

evidence.title: Existing owner-scoped branch returns documented 404
evidence.summary: The same harness selected the existing scoped branch, confirmed a user-and-title query, and received `Book not found!` with HTTP 404 for a cross-owner request.

evidence.title: User deletion is administrator-gated
evidence.summary: The handler resolves the authenticated principal, checks `user.admin`, and calls the deletion model only inside the administrator branch.

evidence.title: Deletion authorization verified with synthetic principals
evidence.summary: Handler tests returned 401 with zero mutation calls for a non-admin; the deletion Subject additionally verified 200 and one deletion call for an administrator.

proposals.title: Enforce ownership at the book handler boundary
proposals.premise: The defect is confined to the `if vuln` title-only branch in `get_by_title`.
proposals.uncertainty: A dependency-backed regression suite is absent, so response compatibility must be encoded in a new local test before correction.
proposals.rationale: Create `tests/test_authorization_regression.py` with synthetic owner, non-owner, non-admin, and admin cases; then replace the conditional lookup with the already-present owner-scoped query. This is the smallest boundary and leaves unrelated educational vulnerability switches unchanged.
proposals.expectedInsight: Demonstrate that cross-owner lookup changes from 200 to the documented 404 while owner retrieval and administrator deletion retain their existing response shapes.

proposals.title: Encode owned-book lookup in the model boundary
proposals.premise: The regression exists because ownership filtering is optional at call sites rather than represented by a dedicated data-access operation.
proposals.uncertainty: A model helper touches a broader boundary than the existing handler-only correction and may be unnecessary for a single consumer.
proposals.rationale: Add a model-level owned-title lookup, require the handler to use it, and test both the model query contract and handler responses using synthetic local data. This tests whether centralizing authorization-resistant data access provides stronger regression prevention.
proposals.expectedInsight: Determine whether a reusable owner-scoped model API materially reduces future unscoped-query risk enough to justify the broader change.
